premise: For 20 years I worked double nursing shifts and poured my late husband’s $190,000 insurance payout into my son-in-law’s dental practice, with a handshake deal that I’d own 25%. Once revenues hit $2 million, he and my daughter forged a stock transfer to erase my stake and had their lawyer send me a cease-and-desist for ‘disparaging the brand.’ I gathered internal billing records, filed a whistleblower complaint over systematic Medicaid overbilling, then sued for constructive fraud. The practice was forced to buy me out for $320,000 in penalties and lost its largest contract — I used the money to open a walk-in dental clinic in the same strip mall that now absorbs half their uninsured patients.
